items.title: Enhance Internal API for Skill Discovery in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces three new internal API endpoints for skill discovery and asset management, enhancing the functionality of the AuthBackend. The new endpoints allow the aichat2 worker to retrieve active skills, update usage timestamps, and generate presigned URLs for asset bundles. Additionally, a management command has been added to validate skill provider references, ensuring data integrity.

items.title: Update Azure/k8s-set-context to v5 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the Azure/k8s-set-context dependency from version 4 to 5, introducing several enhancements including migration to ESM and updates to the Node version from 20 to 24. The changes also include various Dependabot updates to improve security and compatibility across workflows.

items.title: Enhance COS Uploads with Service Prefix in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update ensures that the `openaihk/worker` uploads artifacts to specific service folders in the COS bucket, preventing clutter in the root directory. Key changes include the addition of per-service CDN path constants and a new helper function to enforce valid path prefixes. The modifications improve organization and maintainability of uploaded files.

items.title: Exciting New Skill Model and API Features in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `Skill` model along with a comprehensive REST API for managing skills, including CRUD operations and asset uploads to Tencent COS. Key features include a two-tier storage model for skills, validation for asset uploads, and JWT authentication for secure access. The changes enhance the platform's capability to manage user-generated skills effectively.

items.title: Automated research cycle completed in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The latest automated research cycle has been successfully executed, reviewing 15 candidates with key insights documented in the new report. Notably, all new listings failed to meet liquidity lock or holder concentration requirements, resulting in zero buy intents. The report includes detailed scores and actions for each candidate, providing valuable data for future decisions.

items.title: Revamp Code Execution to Bash with Enhanced Sandbox Features in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new execution tool named 'Bash', replacing the previous 'code_execute' functionality. Key enhancements include environment variable injection, file materialization, and preinstalled provider CLIs, streamlining the execution process for various skills. Additionally, security measures have been strengthened by preventing subprocesses from inheriting the environment, ensuring a more secure runtime environment.

items.title: New Revenue Transactions Added in Roadmap 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two new on-chain transactions to the buyback history feed, both dated 2026-05-01. The changes implement a new revenue allocation rule, allocating 5% of revenue with 80% going to liquidity provision and 20% to buyback. These transactions enhance transparency and tracking of revenue utilization.

items.title: Enhance inviter_id recovery for better user referrals in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a defense-in-depth fix that allows the recovery of the inviter_id from a nested redirect query parameter when it is not available at the top level. This change ensures that user referrals are correctly captured even in cases of legacy or malformed URLs, enhancing the reliability of the authentication process. The fix complements a related change in Nexior, providing additional protection against potential regressions.

items.title: Fix inviter_id binding for custom domains in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves issues with the inviter_id not being preserved during the authentication redirect for custom-domain Nexior deployments. Key changes include replacing the logout action with resetAll to prevent race conditions and ensuring inviter_id is correctly passed in the URL. This fix enhances the referral binding experience for users signing up via OAuth providers like WeChat.

items.title: Feat: add 2026-05-01 buyback and first creator-fee LP add in Roadmap ✨
Scheduled Research Cycle Report Published in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The latest research cycle report for May 1, 2026, has been published, detailing the review of 17 candidates. Notably, all new listing candidates failed security hard gates, leading to 8 skips. The report also highlights the health of data sources and a significant watchlist signal for ORCA, which saw a 31.4% increase in 24 hours.

items.title: Upgrade autoprefixer to version 10.5.0 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps autoprefixer from version 10.4.27 to 10.5.0, introducing support for mask-position-x and mask-position-y. The changes enhance CSS compatibility and improve styling capabilities. Additionally, the dependencies have been updated to ensure optimal performance.

items.title: Automated research cycle completed in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The latest automated research cycle has been successfully executed, adding a report covering nine mainstream watchlist tokens and five new listing candidates, all of which were skipped due to significant red flags. Key data sources reported no errors, ensuring a smooth analysis process. The mainstream snapshot indicates stable conditions for major tokens, with notable movers like BONK and RAY showing positive trends.

items.title: Update React to 19.2.5 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps React from version 19.2.4 to 19.2.5, incorporating important cycle protections for React Server Components. The changes were made in both the package.json and yarn.lock files to ensure consistency across the project. This upgrade enhances stability and performance for our frontend application.

items.title: New Research Cycle Insights in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The latest research cycle for May 1, 2026, has been completed, adding a comprehensive report with 439 new entries. Key findings include 25 candidates reviewed, with 10 holds and 15 skips due to liquidity concerns. Notably, CHILLGOBLI is marked for a hold pending further observation, while several tokens were flagged as suspicious and skipped.
